extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT won the 30.8km ITT by 21 seconds over ASGREEN, while POGACAR rode conservatively to secure his second Tour title by 5:20.
tactical_patterns: POGACAR rode conservatively to avoid crash risk with 5:45 lead, prioritizing survival over stage hunting despite TT strength.
KUNG faded in final third despite leading at second check, revealing insufficient three-week endurance for sustained power efforts.
VAN AERT maintained faster speed through all three time checks showing superior pacing discipline over KUNG's early aggression.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: 51.5kph average speed on rolling TT shows exceptional three-week durability after winning mountain stage
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: Danish TT champion at 51kph average suggests underrated stage racing TTer despite no prior GT stage wins
rider_signals.KUNG Stefan: negative: European champion faded final third losing to ASGREEN after leading second check, poor three-week pacing strategy
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: neutral: Eighth place despite TT strength confirms deliberate risk-minimization with large GC margin to defend
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Third on stage, second overall debut Tour signals strongest Danish GT rider since RIIS era
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: First Ecuadorian Tour podium but unable to challenge second place, confirming ITT as limiting factor
rider_signals.BISSEGGER Stefan: positive: Young Swiss nearly won after first three-week race, early fast time shows emerging TT talent
